title: Scoped accent overrides
tagline: Per-project & per-language color control — your accent now follows your context.

slides.title: Pin an accent to a project
slides.body: Open Settings → Ayu Islands → Accent → Overrides and add the project you're in. Pick any color from the swatch grid or shuffle a contrast-aware random one. Switch IntelliJ windows and each project keeps its own tint — the focus-swap kicks in instantly.

slides.title: Pin an accent to a programming language
slides.body: Same panel, different scope: bind Kotlin → gold, Python → sky, TypeScript → cyan. Whenever a project's dominant language matches and no project override exists, the language accent wins. Stops you guessing which repo is which.

slides.title: Resolution chain you can see
slides.body: Project override beats language override beats global. The Settings panel shows a live Currently active hint so you can tell at a glance which scope produced today's color — no more Apply-and-pray.
